title: I built an agentic trading workflow — AI agents handle the entire analysis pipeline, you handle the execution
author: Tsatsralt
selftext:
I was supposed to launch this today. Then my payment provider told me they can't process international cards. Launching anyway — free plan works fine.

But here's what I actually want to talk about: **agentic trading** — not "AI-assisted" trading where you ask a chatbot questions, but a workflow where AI agents autonomously handle the full analysis and decision-making pipeline.

## What "agentic trading" means in practice

Most AI trading tools are chatbots. You type a question, you get an answer. That's not agentic. That's search with extra steps.

Here's what an agentic workflow looks like:

**You say:** "Analyze EURUSD on the 4H."

**What happens behind the scenes:**

1. A supervisor agent decides this needs both technical and fundamental analysis
2. The technical analyst agent autonomously fetches 200 candles from the 4H, runs RSI/MACD/EMA/ATR, identifies market structure, scans for order blocks and fair value gaps
3. The fundamental analyst agent autonomously checks the economics calendar, scans news for EUR and USD, pulls macro context
4. The supervisor synthesizes both — resolves contradictions, flags risks ("CPI tomorrow — don't enter yet"), and produces a structured recommendation with entry, SL, TP, and R:R
5. If you approve the setup, it becomes a tracked trading signal

**No copy-pasting data. No switching tabs. No re-explaining your strategy.** The agents remember your preferred indicators, timeframes, and risk rules from previous sessions.

And it doesn't stop there — you can spawn autonomous agents that run this analysis on a schedule. Every 30 minutes, every 4 hours, or triggered by economic events. They monitor your watchlist and push notifications when conditions are met.

## What the agents can do

* **Fetch and analyze live market data** — candles, indicators, chart patterns. Real API calls, not hallucinated numbers.
* **Scan news and economics calendar** — catch events you'd forget to check. "Fed speakers Thursday, CPI Friday — hold off on USD positions."
* **Draw on your charts** — the AI draws support/resistance, trendlines, and zones directly on TradingView charts.
* **Propose trades** — structured signals with entry, take-profit, stop-loss, and R:R ratios.
* **Monitor autonomously** — spawned agents watch markets 24/7 and alert you.
* **Remember how you trade** — persistent memory of your style, indicators, risk tolerance across sessions.

## What the agents can NOT do

**They don't execute trades.** You still place orders on your exchange/broker manually. The agents handle the analysis and decision-making workflow — scanning, synthesizing, recommending, monitoring. Execution is yours.

This is intentional. I don't want AI auto-executing. Markets are unpredictable. Models hallucinate. The agentic part is the analysis pipeline; the human part is pulling the trigger.

## How it works under the hood

Multi-agent architecture built on LangGraph. A supervisor routes to specialist agents (technical analyst, fundamental analyst) who have their own tools. The agents can call each other, ask follow-up questions, and the supervisor resolves conflicts.

50+ trading skills (structured knowledge modules) teach the agents specific strategies — ICT, chart patterns, risk management, price action. These are open-source and MIT licensed: [github.com/SKE-Labs/trading-skills](https://github.com/SKE-Labs/trading-skills)

**If you trade a strategy the agents don't know — submit it as a skill.** The repo has contribution guides and templates. I want to see how many strategies the community can teach these agents.

## Try it

Free to try at [embient.ai](https://embient.ai) — no credit card needed.

Launched on Product Hunt today: [https://www.producthunt.com/products/embient-ai](https://www.producthunt.com/products/embient-ai)

Not financial advice. The agents analyze — you decide.

comments.author: Jess_Parker19
comments.body: is this ram locally? I can’t run locally due to trash gpu, is there a way you can use agentic AI and it be speedy and not reliant on specs?

comments.author: MrMcFisticuffs
comments.body: I think the most exciting thing about agentic trading is that I can lose money in the market while also slowly bleeding out to my per token costs. /s

comments.author: DomesticOrca
comments.body:
Unfortunately it crashed for me during setup.

But I think, product wise, what you have here is quite well architected. How long did it take you?

comments.author: dogazine4570
comments.body:
This is a cool direction — the distinction between “AI-assisted” (chat interface) and truly agentic (autonomous pipeline) is important and often glossed over.

A few questions I’d be curious about:

- How are you handling model drift and regime shifts? An agentic system that works in one volatility regime can quietly degrade in another.
- Are the agents specialized (e.g., data ingestion, feature engineering, signal generation, risk modeling), or is it more of a single orchestrated model?
- What kind of guardrails exist between “analysis” and “action”? Even if execution is manual, do you surface confidence intervals, scenario analysis, or just directional signals?

One concern with fully agentic pipelines is overfitting via feedback loops — especially if agents are optimizing based on recent performance metrics. Are you incorporating out-of-sample validation or walk-forward testing automatically in the workflow?

Also, since the execution is still human, how do you prevent cognitive bias from overriding the system at the worst times (e.g., skipping trades after drawdowns)?

I like the framing of “you handle execution” — keeps regulatory and brokerage complexity out of scope. Would be interesting to see how transparent the reasoning chain is. If I can’t audit why the agent produced a signal, I’d personally hesitate to rely on it.

Curious to see a deeper breakdown of the architecture if you’re open to sharing.
